_note: Shipped default for the Ops Hub config page (Notion page titled '⚙️ Hub Config'). Mirrors the [TEMPLATE] Notion Operations Hub schema. Teams reshape this by running hub-setup, never by hand-editing. Every field label starts with an emoji; the same column name uses the same emoji across tables. Body sections each carry a heading + purpose so a team (and the composing skill) knows what each is for. Structural fields (titles + relations) are NOT listed — skills resolve them by role (type/target), not by label. See hub-conventions.md.

stamp.position: first line of the managed body, above the first section

manualNotes.heading: 📝 Manual Notes
manualNotes.text: Use this section for any notes you want to preserve through future context refreshes. The update skill will never touch anything below this heading.

status.label: 📋 Status
status.purpose: Engagement state (select: Prospect / Active / Recently Lapsed / Lapsed / TBC). Set from the most recent client activity (email, meeting, invoice, or task): Prospect = not yet won; Active = activity within the last 3 months; Recently Lapsed = last activity 3 to 12 months ago; Lapsed = no activity for 12+ months; TBC = unknown. Drives Engagement Overview.

main_contact_name.label: 👤 Main Contact Name
main_contact_name.purpose: Primary contact name; mirrors the ⭐ row of the Contacts body table.

hourly_rate.label: 💷 Hourly Rate
hourly_rate.purpose: Default hourly rate for this client (money).

drive_folder.label: 📁 Drive Folder URL
drive_folder.purpose: URL to the client's file folder; the root for relative source locations.

lead_source_detail.label: 🔗 Lead Source Detail
lead_source_detail.purpose: Free-text detail on the lead source.

original_agreement_date.label: 📅 Original Agreement Date
original_agreement_date.purpose: Date the master agreement was signed.

agreement_url.label: 📝 Agreement URL
agreement_url.purpose: Link to the signed master agreement.

company_number.label: 🏢 Company / Charity Number
company_number.purpose: Companies House or charity registration number.

signatory_name.label: ✍️ Signatory Name
signatory_name.purpose: Name of the person who signs contracts.

signatory_position.label: ✍️ Signatory Position
signatory_position.purpose: Job title of the contract signatory (stated on contracts).

invoice_cc.label: 💰 Invoice CC
invoice_cc.purpose: Anyone to copy on invoice emails.

invoice_instructions.label: 💰 Invoice Instructions
invoice_instructions.purpose: PO numbers / references required by the client's invoicing process.

body.heading: 👥 Contacts
body.purpose: Who the client's people are: names, roles, emails, with the primary contact starred.

body.heading: 📈 Engagement Overview
body.purpose: At-a-glance state: status, in-flight projects, open pipeline, active tasks, last invoice.

body.heading: 📚 Engagement History
body.purpose: Completed projects and their values, plus a short narrative of the engagement so far.

body.heading: 🛠 Tech Stack & Other Tools
body.purpose: Tools and platforms the client uses, inferred from available signals.

body.heading: 💬 Communications Log
body.purpose: Recent themes and the last few interactions (emails and meetings).

body.heading: 🎯 Opportunities & Risks
body.purpose: Where there's room to help, and what could go wrong.

body.heading: 📝 Manual Notes
body.purpose: Human-owned notes; never composed or edited by skills.

sources: Linked records: the client's Projects, Tasks, and Pipeline rows (found via the structural relations).
Invoicing: the connected invoicing/accounting source — for Engagement History values and totals.
Email: the connected mailbox — threads with the client's contacts and at the client's domain (default 2-year look-back).
Calendar: the connected calendar — meetings with the client's contacts/domain (default 2-year look-back).
Files: the client's Drive folder (from drive_folder); read any context note at the folder root.

status.label: 📋 Status
status.purpose: Project state (select: Invoiced / Complete, Ready to Invoice, Active, On Hold, Pending). project-update may propose a change.

billing_type.label: 💳 Billing Type
billing_type.purpose: How the project is billed (select: Fixed Fee / T&M).

invoicing_rule.label: 🧾 Invoicing Rule
invoicing_rule.purpose: When invoices are raised (select: Specific Date / On Completion / 50/50 / Monthly).

fee.label: 💷 Fee
fee.purpose: Fixed fee amount (money); set for fixed-fee projects.

hourly_rate.label: 💷 Hourly Rate
hourly_rate.purpose: Hourly rate (money); set for time-and-materials projects.

body.heading: 🧭 Context
body.purpose: What's prompting the work and the boundary of what this project covers.

body.heading: 🎯 Scope
body.purpose: Outcomes at completion, plus the definition of done.

body.heading: 📦 Deliverables
body.purpose: The concrete artefacts and pieces of work handed over.

body.heading: 🚫 Out of scope
body.purpose: What's explicitly not included.

body.heading: 🔗 Assumptions & dependencies
body.purpose: Conditions the price and timeline rest on, and what the client must provide.

body.heading: 🗓 Timeline
body.purpose: Phases, durations, and the go-live anchor.

body.heading: 👥 Project team
body.purpose: Who's running it on each side.

body.heading: 📝 Manual Notes
body.purpose: Human-owned notes; never composed or edited by skills.

sources: The brief or SOW you're given — the primary source for a project.
Linked records: the project's Tasks (via the structural relation) and its Client.
SOW/brief files: the client's folder, Admin subfolder.
Task comments: read via the connector for progress (used by project-update).

status.label: 📋 Status
status.purpose: Task state (select: Backlog / To Do / In Progress / Blocked / Done; new tasks default to Backlog).

due_date.label: 📅 Due Date
due_date.purpose: Target date (date); set only if stated.

body.heading: User story
body.purpose: Who wants this and why, in one line.

body.heading: Background
body.purpose: Context the doer needs.

body.heading: Task description
body.purpose: What to do.

body.heading: Definition of done
body.purpose: How we know it's complete.

body.heading: Useful context
body.purpose: Links, references, anything that helps.

sources: The text you're given: brief, SOW, meeting transcript, or notes.

status.label: 📋 Status
status.purpose: Deal stage (select: In Progress / Quoting / Quoted / Won / Lost / Declined / On Hold; open = not Won/Lost/Declined).

prospect_name.label: 🏢 Prospect Name
prospect_name.purpose: Free-text prospect / company name (used before a Client record exists).

service_type.label: 🛠️ Service Type
service_type.purpose: Type(s) of work (multi-select: Audit / Automation / Training / Maintenance / Other).

date_closed.label: 📅 Date Closed
date_closed.purpose: Date the deal closed, won or lost (date).

sow_url.label: 📄 SOW Doc URL
sow_url.purpose: Link to the SOW for this deal.
